message:
chore(git): Add .venv/ to .gitignore for Python virtual environments

This commit adds Python virtual environment directory to .gitignore to
prevent accidentally committing local development environments.

Change
------
Added to .gitignore:
```gitignore
# Python environments
.venv/
```

Context
-------
With the addition of pyproject.toml and uv.lock for Python dependency
management, developers will create virtual environments using:
```bash
uv sync          # Creates .venv/ and installs dependencies
uv venv          # Explicitly creates .venv/
python -m venv   # Traditional venv creation
```

All of these create a .venv/ directory by default.

Why Ignore .venv/
-----------------
1. **Not source code**: Virtual environments are built artifacts, not code
2. **Platform-specific**: Contains compiled binaries for specific OS/arch
3. **Reproducible**: Can be recreated from pyproject.toml + uv.lock
4. **Large**: Contains all installed packages (can be 100s of MB)
5. **Standard practice**: Every Python project ignores venv directories

Standard Python .gitignore Patterns
------------------------------------
Common patterns for Python virtual environments:
- venv/
- .venv/
- env/
- ENV/
- .virtualenv/

We're using .venv/ because:
- uv's default
- Hidden (. prefix) so doesn't clutter directory listings
- Widely recognized convention
- Modern Python projects standard

Developer Workflow
------------------
```bash
# Setup (one-time or after pulling pyproject.toml changes)
uv sync

# Activate
source .venv/bin/activate  # Linux/macOS
.venv\Scripts\activate     # Windows

# Work with tools
python scripts/my_tool.py

# Deactivate when done
deactivate

# .venv/ is never committed (gitignored)
```

What IS Committed
-----------------
✓ pyproject.toml (project metadata + dependencies)
✓ uv.lock (locked versions for reproducibility)
✗ .venv/ (local virtual environment - IGNORED)

This ensures everyone gets the same dependencies via the lockfile,
but each developer builds their own local environment.

Impact
------
- Prevents accidental commits of virtual environments
- Keeps repository clean and small
- Follows Python community best practices
- No functional change to UBS behavior

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
